title: Order events with logical, not wall, clocks
description: Use Lamport or vector clocks to capture causality between events, because wall-clock timestamps across nodes are unreliable for ordering.
body: Use when you must order or compare events that originate on different nodes. Do not order distributed events by comparing wall-clock timestamps, and do not assume two clocks are synchronized within a meaningful bound. Actions: attach a Lamport counter for a total-ordering proxy, or a vector clock when you must detect concurrency, increment on local events, and merge incoming clocks element-wise. Validation signals include correct happens-before/concurrent classification in a causality test and reproducible ordering independent of clock skew. Risk boundary: ordering by NTP-synced wall clocks reorders causally-dependent events under skew; treat timestamp-based cross-node ordering as a latent bug and prefer logical clocks.
